item.title: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the AI
item.description: Much hub-bub has been made over the ‘new’ specter of AI that is lurking over the corner of every sector of the economy and is apparently going to kill us all . Technological fears are, of course, not new. I doubt most people buy the extermination nonsense, but I do imagine that quite a few are vaguely worried about losing their jobs, or a general loss in employment. These people miss a general point in economics that I will call upon Henry Hazliett to explain.

item.title: Review - The Second Sons Trilogy
item.description: The World: The Second Sons trilogy takes place in a somewhat isolated corner of the world. We really only hear about three or four extant countries throughout the story. This fits because the story is less about conquest and more about intrigue (more about that later). The two main countries would be the island Kingdom of Dhevyn, and Senet. Before the story began, Senet had invaded and essentially vassalized Dhevyn. The first reason for this would be religious.

item.title: Notes - Predictably Irrational, by Dan Ariely
item.description: Full notes can be downloaded here Introduction We are not only irrational, but predictably irrational—that our irrationality happens the same way, again and again. Whether we are acting as consumers, business-people, or policymakers, understanding how we are predictably irrational provides a starting point for improving our decision-making and changing the way we live for the better. Dan Ariely tells us a story: When he was eighteen, he was the victim of an explosion of ‘a large magnesium flare’.

item.title: About Me
item.description: Ghassan Shahzad Contacts Email: ghass.shahzad@gmail.com LinkedIn: LinkedIn GitHub: GitHub Site: Site Location: Lahore, Pakistan Education Abu Dhabi International School; Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates Beaconhouse Newlands; Lahore, Pakistan 2014—2018 Roots IVY Internation School; Lahore, Pakistan 2018— Research Experience No research experience provided. Work Experience No work experience provided. Projects MCQambridge | GitHub MCQambridge is an automated MCQ past paper solving application for Cambridge A-levels & IGCSE, created to simplify the process of checking answers, calculating scores, and percentages.

item.title: Archives
item.description: 2023 June How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the AI · 14 June 2023 Review - The Second Sons Trilogy · 10 June 2023 Notes - Predictably Irrational, by Dan Ariely · 09 June 2023

item.title: Credits
item.description: This website uses some content created by others under permissive licenses. Here is the attribution for these works: Images Header Background Photo by Samuel Ferrara on Unsplash (Unsplash license )

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description: My website and the theme it is based off of are open-source and freely available for you to examine. My website is not commercial, and (as of yet) it does not have ads. It does use cookies and local storage where necessary. I also use Google Analytics to track usage. My site is hosted on Github Pages, and you can find this hosters’ privacy policy here . Access Logs I host this website on Github Pages.
